description: Bright-field (BF) and weak-beam bright-field transmission electron microscopy (TEM) images showing dislocation morphologies within the hot-rolled microstructure of a low-density Fe-15.5Mn-3.95Al-5Ti-2.15B steel containing δ-ferrite (BCC), austenite (FCC), and TiB₂ particles. Subfigures (a), (a₁), and (a₂) focus on δ-ferrite: (a) shows dense dislocation tangles (red arrows) in δ-ferrite grains distant from TiB₂ particles (SAED inset along BCC <001> zone axis, 100 nm scale bar); (a₁) reveals dislocation pile-ups at the δ-ferrite/TiB₂ interface (200 nm scale bar); (a₂) displays detailed dislocation tangles at the same interface under weak-beam conditions with g = 101 diffraction vector (SAED inset, 200 nm scale bar). Subfigures (b), (b₁), and (b₂) focus on austenite: (b) shows sparse dislocation distributions (yellow arrows) in austenite grains away from TiB₂ particles (SAED inset along FCC <011> zone axis, 100 nm scale bar); (b₁) exhibits dislocation pile-ups at the austenite/TiB₂ interface (marked by blue line, 100 nm scale bar); (b₂) shows substantially sparser dislocation tangles at the same interface under weak-beam conditions with g = 11̄1 diffraction vector (SAED inset, 100 nm scale bar). The images demonstrate higher dislocation densities in δ-ferrite compared to austenite, and denser dislocation accumulation at δ-ferrite/TiB₂ interfaces than at austenite/TiB₂ interfaces, consistent with the existence of a coherent/semi-coherent orientation relationship ({110}δ || {1101}TiB₂, <111>δ || <11̄20>TiB₂) at the former but not the latter.
